title: 4 Establishing Policies for FX Drafts
body_text:
4	Establishing Policies for FX Drafts
4.1	Policy on Complying with PCMLTFA
Establish a policy on training staff who use FX Drafts to comply with
PCMLTFA
and
PCMLTF Regulations
. Contact your AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for information. Financial institutions that subscribe to Central 1âs Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
4.2	Policy on Selling Foreign Currency Drafts to Non-Members
If your financial institution chooses to sell foreign currency drafts to non-members, it must establish a policy to limit the risk of such transactions. Your financial institution is less likely to encounter suspicious transactions when dealing with members given that your financial institution has conducted its due diligence and has developed an ongoing relationship with the individual. Therefore, it is recommended that financial institutions do not sell foreign currency drafts to non-members.
If your financial institution chooses to sell foreign currency drafts of $3,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more to a non-member, you must ascertain the identity of the individual and keep a record of the individualâs name and address, the date of the transaction, and the amount received. These records also must indicate whether the amount was received in cash, cheques, travelers cheques, money orders, or other similar negotiable instruments and must identify the issuer, if applicable. This identification requirement also applies if two or more foreign currency drafts totaling $3,000 (Canadian Equivalent) or more are issued.
The risk of dealing with non-members is greater than dealing with members. As such, your financial institution can establish a policy to ascertain the identity of non-members regardless of the amount of the foreign currency draft. Your financial institution can choose to keep a record of all foreign currency drafts for non-members regardless of the amount.
4.3	Policy on Complying with Convera Rules
Establish internal training policies and procedures to ensure that FX Drafts Users comply with Converaâs rules and restrictions, as follows:
Convera screens and monitors all payment transactions consistent with its legal and regulatory obligations.
Convera will not honour draft payments until all compliance concerns are satisfied. At that time, Convera will release a message to the beneficiaryâs financial institution indicating that the draft can be honoured.
Until the message has been released, the draft canât be negotiated.
Unless there is a legitimate reason, Convera does not support the issuance of multiple drafts under $10,000 USD for the same beneficiary on the same day. Additionally, we recommend that you consider if it has any reporting obligations (e.g., the FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report) when a customer requests to divide payments in this manner.
Convera does not permit the issuance of drafts on behalf of a money service business.
Convera does not permit the issuance of drafts where a financial institution is listed as the beneficiary.
Note:	Detailed procedures are available from Convera.
4.4	Policy on Internal Controls for FX Drafts
Financial institutions are responsible for implementing security practices and internal controls for FX Drafts. Policies may include but are not limited to the following:
Limit access to FX Drafts to employees who need it to complete their job requirements. Each financial institution is responsible for designating its own FX Drafts Users.
Ensure that FX Drafts Users receive their one-time Client Centre passwords in a secure and confidential manner (applies to individuals who do not already have a Client Centre username or whose Client Centre password is reset).
Ensure that FX Drafts Users have their own unique user IDs and do not share user IDs.
Prohibit the use of multiple user IDs for a single employee.
Ensure that FX Drafts Users are reviewed on a regular basis and that required action is taken to update or delete user profiles.
Identify and delete FX Drafts Users who
â
are inactive for one year or more,
â
no longer require access to FX Drafts, or
â
are no longer employees of your financial institution.
4.5	Policy on Client Centre Passwords
FX Drafts Users access the application through a password login to the Client Centre. Once the user is successfully authenticated, the FX Drafts option will appear on the userâs Applications page. Secure passwords are necessary to prevent unauthorized access to the computer network. See the User Management Part for more information.
END
